calendarNotes:
Hillsborough County Public Schools begins the 2025-2026 school year on August 11, 2025. Spring Break runs March 16–20, 2025, and the school year ends May 29, 2025. Like South Florida, HCPS starts in early August — well before Labor Day and among the earliest large districts nationally. Hillsborough's spring break aligns with South Florida's three major counties (Miami-Dade, Broward, Palm Beach) in 2025-2026, making late March one of the busiest travel weeks statewide.

Hillsborough County Public Schools observes two uniquely local festivals as no-school days — events rarely seen on school calendars outside the Tampa Bay area. The Florida State Fair (February 12) is observed as a non-student day, tied to the annual fair held in Tampa each February. The Florida Strawberry Festival (March 8) is similarly observed as a non-student day, honoring the Plant City strawberry harvest festival held in neighboring Plant City. Both are significant Hillsborough County traditions; families relocating from other states may not expect these as school holidays and should mark them on their calendars for childcare planning.

Hillsborough County sits within Florida's Gulf Coast hurricane zone. The Tampa Bay area has a unique geography that makes it particularly vulnerable to surge flooding from Gulf hurricanes — storm surge from a direct hit could affect a substantial portion of the metro area. Florida law allows districts to bank up to three makeup days, but significant storm impacts could extend the school year beyond May 28. Families booking late-May travel should use refundable options if there has been major weather disruption during the year. Peak hurricane risk for the Tampa Bay area runs August through October.

The Gasparilla Pirate Festival, Tampa's signature January event, is not an official school holiday — but it is one of the most attended civic events in Florida, drawing over 300,000 people to downtown Tampa and Bayshore Boulevard on the final Saturday of January. While it doesn't affect school attendance directly, major road closures across downtown Tampa, Hyde Park, and Bayshore Boulevard make it impractical to drive into the area for most of the day. Families with Saturday extracurricular activities, sports games, or medical appointments in or near downtown should plan rescheduling around Gasparilla weekend. The date changes slightly each year — check the official Gasparilla schedule in the fall to confirm before committing to downtown plans.
